penetration_cross_sections.visual_description: Dark grey weld bead sitting on top of joint with minimal penetration into base metal, creating a shallow mushroom shape with poor fusion at edges
Dark grey weld bead with smooth teardrop penetration profile, complete fusion into base metal forming ideal bell-shaped cross-section
Dark grey weld bead with excessive penetration creating deep bulbous shape extending far into base metal, indicating burn-through condition

example_welds.label: Good Weld
example_welds.visual_description: Uniform grey weld bead with consistent width, smooth ripple pattern, clean edges with no spatter, even crown height, regular fish-scale appearance

example_welds.label: Voltage Too Low or Wire Feed Too Slow
example_welds.visual_description: Narrow grey weld bead with high crown, irregular stacked-coin appearance, poor tie-in at edges, convex profile with tight ripples

example_welds.label: Voltage Too High or Wire Feed Too Fast
example_welds.visual_description: Wide flat grey weld bead with excessive spatter around edges, irregular ripple pattern, poor penetration profile, scattered dark spots indicating spatter

example_welds.label: Travel Speed Too Fast
example_welds.visual_description: Narrow grey weld bead with pointed ripples, poor edge tie-in, undercut along edges, irregular bead width with gaps between ripples

example_welds.label: Travel Speed Too Slow
example_welds.visual_description: Wide grey weld bead with excessive buildup, overlapping ripple pattern, burn-through potential, excessive crown height

example_welds.label: CTWD Too Long or Wrong Polarity
example_welds.visual_description: Grey weld bead with heavy spatter pattern surrounding bead, irregular penetration, numerous dark circular spots indicating spatter balls, poor arc stability appearance
causes: Contact tip too far from work
corrective_actions: Check Polarity and maintain less than 1/2 inch CTWD

defects.name: porosity
defects.visual_description: Small round holes, pits, or cavities visible on or within the weld bead surface, may appear as scattered pinholes or clustered gas pockets, porous texture
causes: Insufficient shielding gas flow or coverage
CTWD too long allowing air entrainment
corrections: Clean base metal thoroughly before welding

defects.name: spatter
defects.visual_description: Small dark circular spots scattered around weld bead area, varying in size from pinpoint to larger droplets

defects.name: poor_penetration
defects.visual_description: Weld bead sitting on surface with minimal fusion into base metal, visible lack of tie-in at weld edges

defects.name: burn_through
defects.visual_description: Excessive penetration visible as holes or excessive buildup on backside of weld

penetration_cross_sections.visual_description: Dark grey weld bead sitting on top of joint with minimal penetration into base metal, creating a shallow mushroom shape with poor fusion at edges
Dark grey weld bead with smooth teardrop penetration profile, complete fusion into base metal forming ideal bell-shaped cross-section
Dark grey weld bead with excessive penetration creating deep bulbous shape extending far into base metal, indicating burn-through condition

example_welds.label: Good Weld
example_welds.visual_description: Uniform grey metallic weld bead with consistent width, regular ripple pattern, clean edges, even crown height, smooth fish-scale appearance after slag removal

example_welds.label: Current Too Low
example_welds.visual_description: Grey weld bead with high narrow crown, poor tie-in at edges, irregular stacked appearance, insufficient width, convex profile with tight spacing

example_welds.label: Current Too High
example_welds.visual_description: Wide flat grey weld bead with excessive spatter craters, burn-through appearance, irregular surface texture, excessive penetration indicators

example_welds.label: Weld Speed Too Fast
example_welds.visual_description: Narrow grey weld bead with pointed ripples, undercut along edges, insufficient tie-in, irregular bead width with gaps between weld metal and base metal

example_welds.label: Weld Speed Too Slow
example_welds.visual_description: Wide grey weld bead with excessive crown height, overlapping burn patterns, potential burn-through areas, excessive buildup of weld metal

example_welds.label: Arc Length Too Short
example_welds.visual_description: Grey weld bead with irregular ripple pattern, poor bead shape, potential slag inclusions, uneven surface texture with trapped flux areas
causes: Electrode held too close to work piece

example_welds.label: Arc Length Too Long
example_welds.visual_description: Wide irregular grey weld bead with excessive spatter, poor penetration appearance, wandering bead path, inconsistent ripple formation
causes: Electrode held too far from work piece

defects.name: slag
defects.visual_description: Dark coating or inclusions over weld surface, may appear as dark streaks or patches that need to be chipped away to reveal underlying metal

defects.name: undercut
defects.visual_description: Groove or depression along weld toe edges, appearing as dark lines or channels parallel to weld bead

defects.name: poor_penetration
defects.visual_description: Weld bead sitting on surface with minimal fusion, visible lack of tie-in at weld toes

defects.name: burn_through
defects.visual_description: Excessive penetration creating holes or excessive sagging on backside
